turns.text: I can't focus on studying for finals at all. My mind keeps going back to the breakup last week.
It's worse because my ex is in my study group and I have to see them three times a week.
My anxiety meds aren't helping as much as usual. I'm having panic attacks before every study session.
I missed two assignments already because I couldn't get out of bed. My GPA is going to tank.
Have you had students who dealt with this combo of heartbreak and academic stress before?
I tried the breathing exercises my therapist taught me, but they don't work when I'm this overwhelmed.
The worst part is I have to pretend I'm fine in front of my study group so my ex doesn't know I'm struggling.
I keep checking their social media and then hating myself for it. I know it's making things worse.
My psych professor noticed I've been spacing out in class. I'm worried she'll think I don't care about the material.
My roommate suggested I take incompletes for my classes, but I don't want to fall behind.
Do you think I should ask for accommodations through disability services? My anxiety diagnosis is on file.
I can't even look at our old study spots on campus without feeling nauseous. All my routines are ruined.
The breakup made me question if I'm even cut out for this major. We bonded over psych classes.
I'm sleeping 10 hours but still exhausted. My sleep tracker shows I'm not getting deep sleep.
My therapist is booked until after finals. I don't know where else to turn for help right now.
Part of me wants to skip the study group, but I know I'll fail without that structure.
I tried studying at the library but kept seeing couples studying together and it made me cry in the stacks.
Should I tell my professors what's going on? Or will that make me seem unprofessional?
I feel like I'm mourning the relationship and my academic future at the same time.
My hands shake when I try to take practice tests. I've never had physical anxiety symptoms this bad.
I just got an email reminder about the study group tonight and my chest tightened up immediately.
Has anyone ever successfully changed study groups mid-semester? I wonder if that's an option.
I took your suggestion about box breathing but my mind keeps wandering back to my ex's mannerisms during study sessions.
Do you think melatonin would help with the sleep issues or make the brain fog worse?
I found old notes in my textbook from when we studied together last semester. That set off a panic attack.
My roommate suggested I try studying at the 24-hour diner instead of campus. Have you heard if that works for people?
I scheduled a phone consult with campus counseling but the earliest they had is still 5 days away.
The shaking gets worse when I drink coffee but I can't focus without caffeine. Any alternatives?
I just realized our mutual friends have been avoiding me since the breakup. That's making isolation worse.
Should I delete all our shared study materials or is that too extreme right now?
My professor mentioned extensions are possible with documentation but I don't want special treatment.
I tried the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique but still couldn't finish a practice problem set.
Part of me wants to message my ex just to feel normal again but I know that's unhealthy.
The health center gave me a handout on test anxiety but it doesn't address relationship trauma.
I keep having dreams where I show up to exams unprepared. It's affecting my confidence.
Would it be inappropriate to ask the TA to mediate switching study group partners?
I'm worried if I take sleep aids I'll oversleep and miss morning classes.
My parents keep telling me to 'just focus on school' like it's that simple.
I noticed my appetite has completely disappeared. Is that normal with this level of stress?
The thought of seeing my ex at finals terrifies me more than the actual tests.
I tried journaling like my therapist suggested but I just end up writing angry letters to my ex.
There's a meditation workshop at the student union but I'm afraid I'll break down crying in public.
Do you think studying at the library instead of my apartment would help me focus better?
I caught myself checking my ex's Instagram during study breaks and immediately regretted it.
My professor mentioned exam accommodations but I don't want to seem weak by asking for help.
Would it be better to take melatonin or try herbal tea for sleep? I'm scared of prescription meds.
I keep replaying our last argument instead of reviewing my notes. How do I stop this?
The dining hall makes me nauseous now. Should I force myself to eat or try smoothies instead?
I scheduled a meeting with the disability office but I'm worried they'll think my anxiety isn't 'serious enough'.
Why does my chest physically hurt when I think about seeing them again in class?
My study group meets tomorrow and I'm debating whether to make an excuse not to go.
I noticed my hands shake when I try to highlight textbook passages. This never happened before.
Would it be weird to sit in a different row during exams to avoid eye contact?
My therapist suggested box breathing but I panic when I focus on my breathing too much.
I bought stress balls but squeezing them just makes me think about punching walls.
Should I tell my roommate why I've been acting differently or keep pretending I'm fine?
I keep calculating how much study time I've lost to crying and it's making it worse.
The health center gave me a referral for a psychiatrist but the waitlist is 3 weeks long.
Part of me wants to drop the classes we had together but that would delay graduation.
I tried the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique but counting just reminded me of our anniversary date.
The study group wants to meet at our usual cafe but that's where we had our first date.
I tried studying with noise-canceling headphones but the silence makes my thoughts louder.
My professor asked why my last paper was late and I lied about computer problems.
I scheduled all my exams back-to-back to get them over with but now I'm scared I'll crash.
My mom keeps texting 'how's studying going' and I want to scream.
The counseling center suggested joining a grief support group but I don't want to cry in front of strangers.
I made flashcards but keep writing inside jokes we had instead of terms.
My hands shake so badly during practice tests I can't read my own handwriting.
I know I should eat but the dining hall plays 'our song' on their playlist.
My roommate asked if I wanted to watch a movie and I snapped at her for no reason.
I keep refreshing their social media instead of looking at my lecture notes.
The disability office offered note-taking assistance but I'm embarrassed to use it.
My palms sweat so much during lectures that my notebook pages are warping.
I skipped my morning class because the building is next to their dorm.
Part of me hopes I fail just to punish myself for being so distracted.
I tried studying in the library basement where we never went but the pipes keep startling me.
My therapist says I should journal but writing about feelings makes me nauseous.
I changed my phone wallpaper but now every notification reminds me it's changed.
Someone in my class used their perfume today and I had to leave mid-lecture.
I scheduled a meeting with academic advising but I'm scared they'll judge me for falling behind.
I keep refreshing my email to see if academic advising replied yet even though I just sent it an hour ago.
The thought of discussing accommodations makes my throat feel like it's closing up.
I wore noise-canceling headphones to study but kept imagining I heard their laugh through them.
My study group chat is muted but I compulsively check it every 5 minutes anyway.
I tried studying at a coffee shop but panicked when someone ordered their usual drink.
My hands shake so badly I can't underline important textbook passages anymore.
I missed my therapist's call today because I was too anxious to answer about being anxious.
I keep rewriting the same flash card because my handwriting looks so unstable.
The campus health center called to confirm my appointment and I almost canceled right then.
I opened our old photo album 'by accident' when I meant to open lecture slides.
My professor mentioned 'partner projects' and I dissociated for ten minutes straight.
I tried the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique but started counting memories instead of objects.
My roommate asked why I'm taking so many showers and I lied about gym sessions.
I caught myself rereading text threads from three months ago instead of studying.
The dining hall played 'our song' and I left without eating.
I scheduled a makeup exam but now I'm terrified I'll see them at the testing center.
My hands won't stop tingling since I saw someone with their haircut in the quad.
I keep refreshing the class roster page to see if they've dropped the course.
The academic advisor finally replied and now I'm too scared to open the email.
My chest hurts from breathing shallowly all day but deep breaths make me cry.
I tried the grounding technique you suggested but now I'm hyperaware of my heartbeat.
The testing center emailed a seating chart and their name isn't listed - should I feel relieved or paranoid?
My study group chat keeps buzzing and I'm too scared to check if it's them reacting to messages.
I wore noise-canceling headphones to the library but the silence made my thoughts louder.
My mom texted asking why I'm not coming home for spring break and I panicked.
The professor extended the deadline but I feel guilty for needing accommodations.
I opened the advisor's email and it mentioned counseling services - my hands shook so hard I dropped my phone.
My roommate bought my favorite snacks but I can't eat when my stomach is this tight.
I saw their best friend in the psych building and took the stairs to avoid the elevator.
The mindfulness app reminder popped up during a flashback and I threw my tablet.
My therapist's voicemail said she's out sick - now I don't know who to call before my exam.
I scheduled a library carrel but keep imagining them walking past the glass walls.
The tutoring center smells like their cologne and I nearly gagged.
My palms bled from squeezing my stress ball too hard during a practice test.
I muted all group chats but now I'm terrified of missing exam updates.
The disability office wants documentation but I'm ashamed to ask my psychiatrist.
I tried studying at a coffee shop but someone laughed like them and I spilled my drink.
My sleep tracker shows 22 night wakings - how am I supposed to memorize theories like this?
The health center prescribed beta blockers but the bottle says 'may cause nightmares'.
I wrote a draft email to my professor about extensions but deleted it twelve times.
I taped a photo of my psychiatrist's signature to the disability form just to see how it would feel to have it ready.
There's dried eyeliner on my textbook from crying during flashcard drills.
The beta blockers make my hands tingle but my heartbeat still races during practice essays.
My roommate said I've been reciting Freud's defense mechanisms in my sleep.
I scheduled a library carrel furthest from the psychology section where we used to study.
Someone left a granola bar on my desk with the same wrapper they always ate - threw it out shaking.
Drew mind maps in red pen to match the color our professor uses for important concepts.
The academic success coach suggested recording lectures but my voice memo app is full of old couple videos.
Made a 4am panic list of every exam topic I haven't reviewed yet - it's three notebook pages.
Realized I've been chewing the same pen cap they once borrowed and immediately spat it out.
Wore noise-canceling headphones to the dining hall but the hum reminded me of our road trip playlist.
Tried the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding method during a flashback but got stuck counting their freckles instead.
Set phone reminders to breathe that keep popping up during class with embarrassing chime sounds.
Rewrote all my notes in comic sans because our shared google docs used that font.
Found a study group that meets in the chemistry building where we never went together.
Email draft to my TA asking about alternate exam formats has seventeen versions in my sent folder.
Made an 'emergency kit' with earplugs, fidget toys and peppermints that's now too heavy to carry.
Temporarily changed my phone wallpaper to a cognitive distortion chart instead of our hiking photo.
Practiced exam explanations aloud to my reflection until my dorm neighbor knocked about the noise.
Highlighted key terms in fluorescent yellow - the shade they hated when we shared supplies.
Stashed the emergency kit in my locker and just kept the earplugs in my pocket.
Asked the chem building study group if we could sit facing the wall instead of windows.
Deleted three more draft emails after realizing I was over-explaining my accommodations request.
Changed the breathing reminder chime to vibrate mode during lectures.
Found old flashcards from before the relationship - the handwriting looks unfamiliar now.
Turned my desk to face away from where I used to video call them while studying.
Practiced the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique during a study break in the library stacks.
Set my notes to print in grayscale so the yellow highlights wouldn't stand out as much.
Booked a single study carrel instead of our usual shared spot in the psych building.
Realized I've stopped checking their social media first thing when I wake up.

Realized I've been wearing the same sweatshirt for 4 days because it smells like my ex's laundry detergent.
Tried listening to white noise while studying but the static reminds me of our bad phone connection during fights.
Saw my ex's best friend in the quad and had to hide in a bathroom stall until my hands stopped shaking.
Woke up from a nightmare that I failed all my exams because I was too busy grieving to study.
My professor extended my deadline but now I'm crying because I feel like a charity case.
Almost texted my ex when I got a 92 on a quiz because they always knew how to celebrate my wins.
Tried progressive muscle relaxation but started sobbing when I tensed my shoulders - we used to give each other massages.
The dating app notification sound gives me a panic attack even when it's someone else's phone.
Realized I've been absentmindedly chewing my pen cap - it's the same brand he always stole from me.
Cafeteria smells of cinnamon rolls - our Sunday breakfast tradition. Had to leave without eating.
Got lightheaded during lecture when the TA mentioned 'pair projects' - we always partnered up.
Library study carrel still has our initials scratched in the desk. Can't focus there anymore.
Accidentally wore his hoodie to class. Smelled like his cologne and I froze during my presentation.
Tried writing down anxious thoughts but the notebook was our shared grocery list from last month.
Roommate played our breakup playlist 'as a joke' and I vomited from the panic attack.
Keep checking his Spotify to see if he's listening to sad songs about me too.
Calendar notification for our cancelled anniversary dinner popped up during my chem lab.
Failed to recognize my own reflection in the library window - thought it was him behind me.
Tried counting breaths but kept remembering how we'd sync our breathing during cuddles.
Deleted all photos but my hands shook so badly I accidentally archived instead of trashed them.
Overheard someone say 'I love you' on their phone and my knees buckled in the hallway.
Wiped my phone but our shared Notes are still syncing from his device somehow.
Study group suggested meeting at our old coffee shop - had to make an excuse to skip.
Found a receipt in my textbook from when we bought it together freshman year.
Tried the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique but everything I touched reminded me of him.
Slept through my alarm because my dream loop was us having the breakup conversation over and over.
Realized I've been chewing my cuticles raw like I did when we first started dating.
Got a B- on a paper and heard his voice saying 'you're being too hard on yourself' in my head.
Shower steam made the bathroom smell like his soap and I had to get dressed soaking wet to escape it.
Tried writing out a study schedule but kept seeing his handwriting in my notes from when he helped me organize last semester.
My psychopharmacology textbook keeps citing studies about attachment and I have to reread paragraphs multiple times.
Cafeteria was serving his favorite pasta today and I had to leave without eating.
Realized I haven't updated my period tracker since the breakup - missed two cycles from stress.
Got a notification from our shared budgeting app and had a full panic attack in the library.
Took an anxiety pill but it made me too groggy to focus on my cognitive psychology readings.
My roommate asked if I wanted to watch our show and I had to explain we always watched it together.
Found a playlist he made me called 'Study Vibes' and couldn't decide whether to delete it or keep it.
Classmate asked why I wasn't sitting in my usual seat - it's where we first met in Psych 101.
Tried meditating but kept imagining him doing the same guided meditation on his side of campus.
Lab partner mentioned their anniversary and I had to bite my tongue not to trauma dump.
Got an email from the counseling center about group therapy but the time conflicts with my stats review.
Accidentally opened his LinkedIn while checking my own and saw he updated his profile picture.
Took the long route to class to avoid passing the bench where he told me he loved me for the first time.
Realized I've been wearing his hoodie to sleep for 22 nights straight now.
Professor asked about career plans and all I could think was we were supposed to decide that together.
Got a spam text that started the same way he always texted me good morning.
Tried the body scan relaxation technique but my shoulders still feel like concrete from carrying this grief.
Dreamt I aced my exams then woke up crying because he wasn't there to celebrate with me.
Realized my study playlist is just all the songs he introduced me to over the years.
Printed out our study schedule template but couldn't bring myself to write in the exam dates yet.
Kept refreshing my phone hoping he'd text despite knowing he won't, wasted 40 minutes staring at the screen.
Found sticky notes in my textbook with his handwriting from when we studied together last semester.
My chest gets tight every time I pass couples holding hands on campus - it's like physical pain.
Tried replacing his hoodie with a weighted blanket but it doesn't smell like him.
Caught myself drafting messages to him in my head during lecture, missed half the material.
Got an A on my practice quiz but cried because I instinctively turned to tell him first.
The library smells like his cologne today and I don't know if it's real or just my brain.
Realized I haven't eaten properly since the breakup - just enough to stay functional.
Kept zoning out during study group, imagining how he'd explain the concepts in that patient way he had.

Mr. Thompson, thank you for agreeing to speak with me today about the incident at First National Bank.
I understand this might be difficult, but could you tell me what brought you to the bank that afternoon?
I see you're hesitant. Let me assure you that anything you share will be kept confidential.
You were seen on security footage entering the bank at approximately 2:15 PM. What was your reason for visiting?
Just routine questions - were you there to make a deposit, withdrawal, or something else?
I notice you're avoiding eye contact. Is there something about that day that makes you uncomfortable?
Did you notice anything unusual when you first entered the bank? People acting strangely, perhaps?
Even small details could help. What do you remember about the teller who assisted you?
Where exactly were you standing when the commotion started? By the counters? Near the entrance?
You mentioned not seeing much, but surely you heard something. What caught your attention first?
The robbers were wearing masks, correct? Can you describe the style or color of any masks you saw?
Did you notice if any of the robbers had distinguishing features - height, build, tattoos maybe?
I have to ask - is there any reason you might be afraid to share what you saw that day?
Let me rephrase: what part of this experience has been most difficult for you to process?
Security footage shows you moving toward the back of the bank when the robbery began. Why was that?
Did you recognize any of the robbers' voices? Even if you're not sure, every detail helps.
How long would you say the robbery lasted from your perspective?
After the robbers left, do you remember if they went toward the parking lot or down the street?
Is there anything about that day that's been replaying in your mind, even if it seems insignificant?
Before we continue, would you like to take a short break? I want you to feel comfortable.
You mentioned earlier that you didn't see much - but let's focus on what you did observe, even if it was just a glimpse.
Can you describe the clothing of the person closest to you during the robbery?
Between the time you entered the bank and when the robbery began - did anything unusual catch your attention?
Were there any distinctive smells that stood out to you during the incident?
You moved toward the back - was that a conscious decision or more of a reflex?
As you were moving, did you see where the other customers were positioned?
Try closing your eyes for a moment - what's the first sound you remember hearing after the robbery began?
Was there any moment when you felt the robbers were particularly focused on something specific?
Let's talk about the getaway - did you hear any vehicle sounds that might indicate what they were driving?
When the police arrived afterward, did anything the officers said or did trigger a memory for you?
Think back to just before the robbery - was the bank unusually busy or quiet for that time of day?
Do you recall anyone else in the bank who seemed unusually calm or familiar with the situation?
When you replay the events in your mind, is there any moment that feels clearer than the others?
Have you had trouble sleeping since the incident? Sometimes dreams can reveal details we don't consciously remember.
Did you notice if the robbers interacted with each other in any particular way during the robbery?
When you think about the robbers' hands - do you remember seeing any distinctive jewelry or marks?
Was there a point when you thought the situation might turn violent? What made you feel that way?
After the robbers left, how long did it take before you realized it was safe to move?
Have you remembered anything new since we started talking today?
Let's try something different - if you had to pick one word to describe the atmosphere during the robbery, what would it be?
Earlier you mentioned the robbers wore masks - can you describe the material or texture you noticed on them?
I know this is difficult, but if you had to estimate, how many steps away were you from the nearest robber during the incident?
When they were speaking, did their voices sound altered in any way - like they were trying to disguise them?
Think about the moment they entered - was there any hesitation in their movements, or did they seem to know exactly where to go?
